resources.string: Quick help before recording on race day
Manual focus, center line and slow motion
Shutter speed and ISO for sharp bibs
Frame-by-frame and zoom on the phone

string-array.name: quick_start_topic_controls_bullets
string-array.item: The main screen is designed for fast race-day access.
General camera settings open from the top-left settings button.
Photo Finish settings include focus mode, frame rate and center line options.
Shutter speed and ISO are available from the fast exposure button.
The stand-by button reduces battery drain and heat between arrivals.
The bottom sliders control manual focus and zoom.

string-array.name: quick_start_topic_finishline_bullets
string-array.item: Use manual focus to prevent autofocus hunting.
Align the virtual center line with the real finish line.
Choose 240 fps when close decisions must be resolved.
Lower frame rates are enough when the goal is bib verification only.
The center line is visual only and is not burned into the video.

string-array.name: quick_start_topic_exposure_bullets
string-array.item: Fast-moving athletes need fast shutter speed for readable details.
For cycling, around 1/1000 s is a good starting point.
For running, around 1/800 to 1/1000 s is often suitable.
Adjust ISO to available light instead of slowing shutter too much.
A slightly darker image is often better than blown highlights.

string-array.name: quick_start_topic_review_bullets
string-array.item: Use the built-in frame-by-frame viewer when no computer is available.
Play, step left/right and timeline slider find decisive segments quickly.
The frame number and time stay visible during review.
Zoom helps inspect very close finish-line crossings and bib numbers.
With a computer available, review clips on a larger screen while recording continues.
